articles.title: Bombay Stock Exchange highlights role of dharma in Viksit Bharat
articles.summary: Religious and corporate leaders convened at the Bombay Stock Exchange to discuss dharma's role in building a developed India. Speakers emphasized ethics in business, cultural strengthening, and service to humanity. The event, presided over by the Sankaracharya of Kanchi Kamakoti Peetam, highlighted the need for non-political leadership and enduring values for national progress.
articles.content: Bombay Stock ExchangeBy Meghna RewachandMUMBAI: Within the storied precinct of theBombay Stock Exchange(BSE) on Tuesday, an assembly of religious and corporate leaders gathered to reflect on the role of dharma in developing Viksit Bharat.'Dharmam Chara', organised by BSE and Sri Pratyaksha Trust, was presided over by the Sankaracharya of Kanchi Kamakoti Peetam, Swami Vijayendra Saraswathi. Dharmam Chara is a project initiated by Kanchi Peetam which invites religious scholars, chartered accountants, lawyers, doctors and judges for discussion.Sundararaman Ramamurthy, MD & CEO, BSE, delivered a welcome speech in Sanskrit and recited shlokas.He urged the audience to "speak the truth and walk the path of dharma."KR Sekar, partner, Deloitte, said, "The Sankaracharya's guiding principle is that there should be dharma in business. But there cannot be business in dharma." He displayed the map of India and listed various activities carried out by the Kanchi trust.Former SEBI chairman M Damodaran, present chairman of Excellence Enablers, spoke about the "strengthening of culture and dharmic principles towards Viksit Bharat".He equated dharmic principles with corporate governance. Arguing that the country cannot achieve developed nation status by 2047 without grounding its progress in ethical and religious learning, he called on every citizen to transcend short-term gains.Former SBI chairperson Arundhati Bhattacharya, who is now president & CEO, Salesforce -- South Asia, addressed the 'confluence of dharma, values and women empowerment'. She said dharma was about "doing good for humanity". Each time she had wanted to give up during her career, there was someone who stood by her side and encouraged her, said Bhattacharya, terming this an act of dharma. She urged the audience to ask themselves, "Did I enable someone today or did I burden them with guilt?"R Shankar Raman, president, Larsen & Toubro, spoke about 'social responsibility from a corporate perspective'.He said CSR funds could be channelised effectively in myriad ways, noting that rural India deserves equal attention. Raman said that the process of migration generates disparity in income and well being which gives rise to communal fissures. He ended by saying service to humanity is service to God, and all corporates must contribute.V Shankar, chairman of SIES, mapped dharma onto India's economic canvas, saying 18 languages, 6,400 castes, 28 states and eight union territories are rolled into one Bharat.Shankaracharya Vijayendra Saraswathi said, "It is good that Bombay Stock Exchange is exchanging ideas and feelings too. We need a level of non-political leadership to execute tasks that govt cannot or may not want to carry out. We require ethics in business, but business should not exist in religion. Satyamev Jayate remains the only enduring currency in Viksit Bharat."You Can Also Check:Gold Rate in Mumbai|Silver Rate in Mumbai|Bank Holidays in Mumbai|Public Holidays in Mumbai|Mumbai AQI|Weather in Mumbai Hot PicksKerala VotingRaghav ChadhaPurple cap winnerOrange cap winnerIPL Points TablePublic holidays April 2026Bank Holidays April Kerala VotingRaghav ChadhaPurple cap winnerOrange cap winnerIPL Points TablePublic holidays April 2026Bank Holidays April Top TrendingPope Leo XIVMamata BanerjeeTop Stock RecommendationsCrude Oil PriceMike VrabelMegan Thee StallionSerena WilliamsIPL Points TableSchool Holidays in AprilKarnataka 2nd PUC Exam Result Date Pope Leo XIVMamata BanerjeeTop Stock RecommendationsCrude Oil PriceMike VrabelMegan Thee StallionSerena WilliamsIPL Points TableSchool Holidays in AprilKarnataka 2nd PUC Exam Result Date Tired of too many ads?go ad free nowTrending StoriesIn CityEntire WebsiteâYou canât talk like this,â observer shoots back at CEC Gyanesh Kumar, gets removedKerala Election 2026 Live Updates: CM Pinarayi Vijayan, BJP state chief K Surendran among early voters; articles.title: Manori desal plant clears CRZ hurdle, Centre’s nod awaited
articles.summary: The Maharashtra Coastal Zone Management Authority has recommended a proposed desalination plant at Manori for CRZ clearance, moving it closer to Union environment ministry approval. The 400 MLD plant aims to address Mumbai's water scarcity, with conditions including mangrove protection and minimal impact on fishing activities. Mitigation measures are in place to manage brine discharge and protect the marine ecosystem.
articles.content: MUMBAI: The proposed desalination project at Manori has moved a step closer, with the Maharashtra Coastal Zone Management Authority (MCZMA) recommending it for Coastal Regulation Zone (CRZ) clearance, subject to conditions.The clearance came at a March 25 meeting, with the authority passing on the project file to the Union environment ministry for approval.BMC plans to set up a 400 MLD seawater desalination plant at Manori, with 200 MLD to be taken up in Phase I. An official said that with Mumbaiâs rising population and increasing water demand, desalination remains one of the few viable longterm options to address water scarcity in the suburban region.As per project documents, the site falls across CRZ-I(B), CRZ-III, and CRZ-IV categories, with a small portion within a 50-metre mangrove buffer zone. The project will use a two-stage desalination process â seawater reverse osmosis (SWRO) followed by brackish water reverse osmosis (BWRO) â along with an energy recovery system and cleaning infrastructure.Marine work includes laying intake and outfall pipelines, installing diffuser systems on the seabed and discharging treated brine into the sea.While granting its recommendation, the MCZMA imposed several conditions, including strict adherence to CRZ norms, protection of mangroves, ensuring that tidal flow is not obstructed during pipeline laying and implementing robust measures to manage brine discharge.BMC has also been asked to ensure that local fishing activities are not affected and that all debris from construction is scientifically processed outside CRZ areas.The environment impact assessment (EIA) report flagged potential impacts on the marine ecosystem, particularly due to brine discharge. It stated that with proper diffuser systems, the increase in salinity would be limited to slightly above ambient seawater levels, minimising ecological damage within a confined mixing zone.Experts in the authority raised concerns over the impact on marine life and fishing activity. In response, BMC said mitigation measures have been built into the design. These include multiple diffuser ports for rapid dilution, careful selection of discharge locations and maintaining adequate distance between intake and outfall points to prevent recirculation. The authority also said that no desalination activity is proposed within the Manori creek, a key fishing zone, following consultations with local fishing communities.You Can Also Check:Gold Rate in Mumbai|Silver Rate in Mumbai|Bank Holidays in Mumbai|Public Holidays in Mumbai|Mumbai AQI|Weather in Mumbai Hot PicksKerala VotingRaghav ChadhaPurple cap winnerOrange cap winnerIPL Points TablePublic holidays April 2026Bank Holidays April Kerala VotingRaghav ChadhaPurple cap winnerOrange cap winnerIPL Points TablePublic holidays April 2026Bank Holidays April Top TrendingPope Leo XIVMamata BanerjeeTop Stock RecommendationsCrude Oil PriceMike VrabelMegan Thee StallionSerena WilliamsIPL Points TableSchool Holidays in AprilKarnataka 2nd PUC Exam Result Date Pope Leo XIVMamata BanerjeeTop Stock RecommendationsCrude Oil PriceMike VrabelMegan Thee StallionSerena WilliamsIPL Points TableSchool Holidays in AprilKarnataka 2nd PUC Exam Result Date Tired of too many ads?go ad free nowTrending StoriesIn CityEntire WebsiteâYou canât talk like this,â observer shoots back at CEC Gyanesh Kumar, gets removedKerala Election 2026 Live Updates: CM Pinarayi Vijayan, BJP state chief K Surendran among early voters; articles.title: Mumbai doctor performs remote robotic surgery on woman in Oman
articles.summary: In a historic leap for healthcare, a 54-year-old woman in Oman underwent pioneering remote robotic surgery to tackle suspected kidney cancer. The operation, skillfully orchestrated from Mumbai, took place over a staggering distance of 1,500 kilometers.
articles.content: MUMBAI: A 54-year-old woman from Oman with suspected kidney cancer underwent a remote robotic surgery on Tuesday, with the operating surgeon controlling the procedure from Mumbai while the patient was over 1,500 km away in Muscat."A team of urologists from our hospital was stationed at Medical City Muscat Hospital, a military hospital, ready to physically intervene if needed, but the remote surgery went off without a hitch," said Dr T B Yuvaraja, director of uro-oncology and robotic surgery from Kokilaben Dhirubhai Ambani Hospitals, Andheri, who performed the surgery from Mumbai.According to the hospital, this is the first cross-border remote robotic surgery performed from India.In Dec 2025, Dr Yuvaraja operated on a patient in Mumbai from Shanghai, about 8,000 km away. Dr Yuvaraja said, "What we are witnessing is a shift in how healthcare will be delivered globally." He said the ability to operate across borders in real time means that access to the best medical expertise is not defined by geography.The collaboration came about after the military hospital in Muscat, which had recently acquired a robotic arm, was looking for a technical partnership with another hospital in robotic surgery."The company that manufactured the arm brought us together, with our team performing a surgery in Muscat on another patient on Monday. The next day, we decided to do the remote operation from Mumbai," said the doctor. Studies estimate that roughly five billion people worldwide do not have access to safe and affordable surgical care; telerobotic surgery is being promoted to improve surgical access.The number of telerobotic surgeries has been increasing, with hospitals using robotic arms across their various branches. SS Innovations International, maker of the indigenous SSII Mantra robotic system, recently said it had completed 100 robotic telesurgeries. However, use of the robotic arm increases the cost of a surgery by Rs 1 lakh to Rs 2 lakh. The doctors say that robotic precision helps reduce the hospital stay and recovery time for the patient.You Can Also Check:Gold Rate in Mumbai|Silver Rate in Mumbai|Bank Holidays in Mumbai|Public Holidays in Mumbai|Mumbai AQI|Weather in Mumbai Hot PicksKerala VotingRaghav ChadhaPurple cap winnerOrange cap winnerIPL Points TablePublic holidays April 2026Bank Holidays April Kerala VotingRaghav ChadhaPurple cap winnerOrange cap winnerIPL Points TablePublic holidays April 2026Bank Holidays April Top TrendingPope Leo XIVMamata BanerjeeTop Stock RecommendationsCrude Oil PriceMike VrabelMegan Thee StallionSerena WilliamsIPL Points TableSchool Holidays in AprilKarnataka 2nd PUC Exam Result Date Pope Leo XIVMamata BanerjeeTop Stock RecommendationsCrude Oil PriceMike VrabelMegan Thee StallionSerena WilliamsIPL Points TableSchool Holidays in AprilKarnataka 2nd PUC Exam Result Date Tired of too many ads?go ad free nowTrending StoriesIn CityEntire WebsiteBaramati bypoll: Congress withdraws to pave way for Sunetra Pawar, 30 independent candidates still in frayâYou canât talk like this,â observer shoots back at CEC Gyanesh Kumar, gets removedKerala Election 2026 Live Updates: CM Pinarayi Vijayan, BJP state chief K Surendran among early voters; articles.title: AI-powered air purification debuts in Mumbai realty
articles.summary: Superb Realty is deploying an AI-based air purification system across over 1 million sq ft of its developments, partnering with deep-tech firm Praan. This initiative marks a significant shift in real estate, integrating environmental quality as a key determinant of asset value and occupant well-being.
articles.content: MUMBAI: In a first for the cityâs real estate sector, an AI-based air purification system is set to be deployed across a large-scale development, with Superb Realty partnering deep-tech firm Praan to roll out intelligent indoor air infrastructure spanning over 1 million sq ft.The deployment will begin at the developerâs commercial project and will be scaled across upcoming residential and mixed-use developments in Mumbai. The move signals a shift in how developers are integrating environmental quality into core design and infrastructure, beyond traditional metrics such as location and amenities.Industry experts note that indoor environmental quality is increasingly emerging as a measurable determinant of asset value, productivity and long-term health outcomes.âAir quality is fast becoming a quantifiable premium in real estate,â said Shilpin Tater, adding that data now links cleaner indoor air to improved cognitive performance and occupant well-being.Praanâs proprietary system combines real-time environmental sensing with AI-led optimisation and adaptive purification. According to the company, the technology can remove ultrafine particles significantly smaller than conventional filtration benchmarks and eliminate harmful gases at faster rates, enabling buildings to dynamically respond to fluctuating indoor and outdoor air conditions.âAir remains one of the least optimised systems in building design despite being the most frequently used interface,â said Angad Daryani.The initial rollout at Superb Altura will serve as a pilot, creating a benchmark for future deployments across the companyâs portfolio. The model has been designed for scalability, allowing integration across a wider pipeline of projects.Global studies indicate that individuals spend nearly 90% of their time indoors, where air can be 2â5 times more polluted than outdoors due to particulate matter, volatile organic compounds and microbial pollutants.The World Health Organization has repeatedly flagged air pollution as a leading environmental health risk, with India housing several of the worldâs most polluted urban centres.With urban density and pollution levels rising, developers are increasingly focusing on wellness-oriented infrastructure, particularly in premium housing and Grade A commercial spaces. The initiative positions Superb Realty at the intersection of real estate, health and climate technology, reflecting a broader shift towards data-driven, occupant-centric urban development.You Can Also Check:Gold Rate in Mumbai|Silver Rate in Mumbai|Bank Holidays in Mumbai|Public Holidays in Mumbai|Mumbai AQI|Weather in Mumbai Hot PicksKerala VotingRaghav ChadhaPurple cap winnerOrange cap winnerIPL Points TablePublic holidays April 2026Bank Holidays April Kerala VotingRaghav ChadhaPurple cap winnerOrange cap winnerIPL Points TablePublic holidays April 2026Bank Holidays April Top TrendingPope Leo XIVMamata BanerjeeTop Stock RecommendationsCrude Oil PriceMike VrabelMegan Thee StallionSerena WilliamsIPL Points TableSchool Holidays in AprilKarnataka 2nd PUC Exam Result Date Pope Leo XIVMamata BanerjeeTop Stock RecommendationsCrude Oil PriceMike VrabelMegan Thee StallionSerena WilliamsIPL Points TableSchool Holidays in AprilKarnataka 2nd PUC Exam Result Date Tired of too many ads?go ad free nowTrending StoriesIn CityEntire WebsiteâYou canât talk like this,â observer shoots back at CEC Gyanesh Kumar, gets removedKerala Election 2026 Live Updates: CM Pinarayi Vijayan, BJP state chief K Surendran among early voters; articles.title: Energy boost for India: LPG carrier ‘Green Asha’ reaches Mumbai safely via Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: India-flagged LPG carrier Green Asha safely docked at JNPA, carrying 15,400 tonnes of LPG. This marks the first arrival since the West Asian conflict, navigating the Strait of Hormuz. The vessel's safe passage boosts India's energy security and helps stabilize domestic supplies amid geopolitical risks.
articles.content: MUMBAI: In a significant boost to Indiaâs energy security amid escalating tensions in West Asia, the India-flagged LPG carrier Green Asha docked safely at Jawaharlal Nehru Port Authority (JNPA) on Tuesday, becoming the first such vessel to reach the port since the outbreak of the ongoing conflict involving Iran, the United States and Israel.Carrying 15,400 tonnes of LPG, the vessel navigated the strategically critical Strait of Hormuzâa chokepoint for global energy suppliesâbefore berthing at JNPAâs liquid terminal, operated by Bharat Petroleum Corporation Limited and Indian Oil Corporation Limited. Officials confirmed that the vessel, its cargo and crew are safe, underlining the resilience of maritime logistics even amid heightened geopolitical risks.WatchGreen Asha Tanker Delivers Over 15,000 Tonnes Of Lpg To IndiaThe development comes at a time when concerns over disruptions in LPG supplies have caused anxiety among households and commercial users, particularly in Mumbai and across Maharashtra, where dependence on imported fuel remains high. Industry experts said the successful arrival will help stabilise supplies, ease pressure on domestic distribution networks and potentially prevent sharp price rises.JNPA, Indiaâs premier container and liquid cargo gateway, has in recent years expanded its capacity to handle energy imports, making it a critical node in the countryâs fuel supply chain.With nearly a fifth of the worldâs oil and gas trade passing through the Strait of Hormuz, any disruption in the corridor has immediate ripple effects on Indiaâs economy.PollDo you think the arrival of Green Asha will mitigate the LPG supply concerns in Mumbai?Yes, absolutelyNo, it won't change muchExperts say the voyage of Green Asha signals growing confidence in Indiaâs shipping and port infrastructure to withstand global uncertainties. Going forward, such voyages could become both more frequent and more strategically significant as India diversifies supply routes, strengthens its merchant fleet and builds resilience against prolonged geopolitical crises.You Can Also Check:Gold Rate in Mumbai|Silver Rate in Mumbai|Bank Holidays in Mumbai|Public Holidays in Mumbai|Mumbai AQI|Weather in Mumbai Hot PicksKerala VotingRaghav ChadhaPurple cap winnerOrange cap winnerIPL Points TablePublic holidays April 2026Bank Holidays April Kerala VotingRaghav ChadhaPurple cap winnerOrange cap winnerIPL Points TablePublic holidays April 2026Bank Holidays April Top TrendingPope Leo XIVMamata BanerjeeTop Stock RecommendationsCrude Oil PriceMike VrabelMegan Thee StallionSerena WilliamsIPL Points TableSchool Holidays in AprilKarnataka 2nd PUC Exam Result Date Pope Leo XIVMamata BanerjeeTop Stock RecommendationsCrude Oil PriceMike VrabelMegan Thee StallionSerena WilliamsIPL Points TableSchool Holidays in AprilKarnataka 2nd PUC Exam Result Date Tired of too many ads?go ad free nowTrending StoriesIn CityEntire WebsiteBaramati bypoll: Congress withdraws to pave way for Sunetra Pawar, 30 independent candidates still in frayâYou canât talk like this,â observer shoots back at CEC Gyanesh Kumar, gets removedKerala Election 2026 Live Updates: CM Pinarayi Vijayan, BJP state chief K Surendran among early voters; articles.title: Firing at RTI activist: Stolen motorcycle used by shooter found
articles.content: Mumbai: The crime branch unit-1 recovered the motorcycle used in the Airoli firing in which RTI activist Amit Maurya, 40, sustained a bullet injury near his neck on Monday night. The bike was found on the roadside in Mumbra, reports George Mendonca.On Monday, at around 11.45 pm, Maurya and his friend Sandeep Gavas, 44, were standing near the parked MUV of Gavas when a biker wearing a helmet approached them and, brandishing a pistol, fired in their direction. As they dodged, two bullets hit the MUV's windscreen. Maurya and Gavas ran when the assailant fired two more rounds. One bullet pierced Maurya's neck near the left ear, while the other bullet fired at Gavas hit a wall on the roadside.A police source said, "The CCTV camera footage was scanned, and the shooter riding the bike was tracked. As he was wearing a helmet, he could not be identified. However, the bike's registration number was caught on CCTV. It was found abandoned in Mumbra in the wee hours of Wednesday. From the bike's registration number, the owner's details were obtained from the RTO. It was learnt that the bike was stolen from Sanpada."Maurya underwent an operation at Fortis Hospital, Mulund, and the bullet lodged near his neck was removed. Police said his condition was stable and he was shifted from the ICU to the general ward. A police officer privy to the case, said, "As of now, it cannot be confirmed if it was a contract killing. articles.title: Cooking up change: Commercial kitchens turn to solar & biomass
articles.content: In the 1980s, Nagesh Nayak, owner of Cafe Mysore in Matunga, rigged up a contraption more commonly found on factory floors. He installed a gas-powered boiler to pipe pressurised steam to his cooking range.As the steam condensed back into water in these vessels, it cooked food faster and more evenly.It wasn't fuel scarcity that motivated Nagesh, an electrical engineer-turned-restaurateur, to innovate. Dagadi kolsa, or rock coal, that fired his burners was plentiful. He simply wanted to reduce his use of the smoky, polluting fuel.Forty years later, his son Naresh has raised the bar on clean cooking all the way to the roof.Solar panels on the terrace generate energy that feeds into the grid, which in turn helps discount his bills. Indirectly, "solar energy powers about 50% of our cooking," says Naresh. Piped natural gas takes care of the rest, including more energy-intensive processes such as deep-frying. "Eventually," he says, "nearly everything will run on electricity."According to the Institute for Energy Economics and Financial Analysis, electricity-based devices can be 14% cheaper than PNG and 37% cheaper than non-subsidised LPG.Renewables reduce the bill further. Fuel diversification, says Naresh, "is a safeguard against a future where infrastructural risks and fuel shortages are real risks to a business." That safeguard is gaining ground across the food service sector, as kitchens explore more resilient alternatives to LPG.Saili Jahagirdar, proprietor of Zillionth Bistro in Pune, discovered pellet stoves. These burn pellets of compressed agricultural residue such as rice, soybean and corn husk.Such stoves aid energy self-reliance and also tackle the waste problem. When her gas supply was cut last month, Jahagirdar sourced two single-burner cookstoves and 20 kg of agri-waste pellets from Samuchit Enviro Tech. These, along with four large induction burners, have kept her operations going.The shift came with a learning curve. The pellet stove, for instance, had to be refuelled every 30 minutes, demanding careful planning to use the flame efficiently."The 10kg stove has a longer fuel cycle, but those were sold out," says Jahagirdar, whose outlay was Rs 75,000 for the stoves and about Rs 100 a day for pellets. Cooking on a pellet stove can take longer than on LPG due to its lower energy output and operating temperature."But we've noticed that slow cooking improves the quality of the food ânoodles are bouncier, and rice is fluffier," Jahagirdar says. She plans to continue using induction and pellet stoves even after LPG supply normalises.Biomass is also the fuel of choice at the ElringKlinger Automotive Components plant in Pune, where the canteen runs on two industrial-grade pellet stoves. Tejas Rajendra Shelke, a junior purchase executive, says the stoves have been working without a hitch, turning out meals of pulao and a vegetable dish for the facility's 2,000 workers across three shifts. The kitchen serves four meal slots a day, each catering to 250â300 people.The surge in adoption is already straining supply chains. Piyush Himmatbhai Patel, owner of Manpacking Industries, a biofuels company in Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar, says biomass pellets are now in short supply as customers who used to buy 5 kilos of the fuel are now buying 5 tonnes.Others up the supply chain are also feeling the strain. Ecosense Appliances, which manufactures biofuel stoves, has added a new production shift.They've received 500 inquiries since early Marchâfrom large hotel chains to small cafÃ©s and canteensâand sold 350 stoves this past month. They usually sell 5-10 a month. "For years, we tried to position pellets as a sustainable alternative to LPG, but without success," says director Ketaki Kokil.She adds the surge may be temporaryâlikely to last only until LPG supply stabilisesâbut it has sparked a wider conversation on alternative fuels.The crisis could prove to be a turning point for cleaner energy in kitchens, agrees Priyadarshini Karve, founder and MD of Samuchit Enviro Tech who has been advocating alternate fuels for decades. "With LPG, we put all our eggs in one basket. At the same time, we're contributing to global warming since LPG is a fossil fuel," she says. "We need to integrate clean, alternative energy sources into our cooking for better energy security."You Can Also Check:Gold Rate in Mumbai|Silver Rate in Mumbai|Bank Holidays in Mumbai|Public Holidays in Mumbai|Mumbai AQI|Weather in Mumbai Hot PicksShreyas Iyer SisterUpdated IPL Points TablePurple cap winnerOrange cap winnerIPL Points TablePublic holidays April 2026Bank Holidays April Shreyas Iyer SisterUpdated IPL Points TablePurple cap winnerOrange cap winnerIPL Points TablePublic holidays April 2026Bank Holidays April Top TrendingTaylor Swift and Travis Kelce RelationshipIPL 2026Donald TrumpB V NagarathnaRomarioMHT CET Admit cardBengal PollIPL Points TableSchool Holidays in AprilKarnataka 2nd PUC Exam Result Date Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce RelationshipIPL 2026Donald TrumpB V NagarathnaRomarioMHT CET Admit cardBengal PollIPL Points TableSchool Holidays in AprilKarnataka 2nd PUC Exam Result Date Tired of too many ads?go ad free nowTrending StoriesIn CityEntire WebsiteED attaches properties worth Rs 944 cr of Piyush Colonisers Ltd, ex-promotersSUV rams IndiGo plane engine at Kolkata airport; aircraft groundedTraffic jam in the jungle: Viral video shows tiger surrounded by vehicles at Ranthambore National ParkIndian Student Dies After Crash: Former NASA intern Nilam Shinde dies in US; kin consent to organ donation'Return my son': Pune woman kills 11-month-old baby to start 'new life'; held after husband asks for childAxe-wielding anti-mining Odisha tribals clash with police; 58 cops among 70 injured in stir against roadUP woman lodges FIR after in-laws seek Rs 30L dowry, kidneyJapanese tourist molested in Jaipur: 1 arrested, minor detained; hunt on for 3 others'Derogatory, communal': Delhi HC asks Centre, police to take action again

articles.title: BMC corporator slams lack of safety measures at Goregaon-Mulund Link Road project site on Film City Road in Mumbai
articles.content: Mumbai: At BMC's standing committee meeting on Wednesday, BJP corporator Priti Satam, through a point of order, highlighted non-adherence to basic safety measures at the Goregaon-Mulund Link Road (GMLR) project site on Film City Road, creating dangerous conditions for passersby and on-site workers.Blaming officials and the contractor for being lackadaisical, Satam pointed out the girders erected at the site have no safety measures around them, debris is dumped at several locations, and there is no proper barricading around the ditches that are filled with stagnant water, thus posing a health risk. "They have not deployed anti-pollution misting units, besides there is no traffic warden," said Satam, who demanded to know what action would be taken against the contractor and officials concerned if an accident took place at the site.Civic officials pointed out BMC has till date imposed a total penalty of Rs 3 crore on the contractor, including the Rs 50 lakh fine slapped in March for the delay in construction of the flyover between Dindoshi court to Dadasaheb Film City, which is part of the GMLR project. Additional municipal commissioner Abhijit Bangar said that safety related issues will be dealt with seriously and necessary action will be taken.Bangar also said that other issues highlighted by Satam would be looked into and addressed.The GMLR project is proposed in four phases and Phase 3 (A) includes the flyover and elevated rotary construction. articles.title: Drunk man from Navi Mumbai detained for hoax bomb blast threat
articles.content: Navi Mumbai: The APMC police detained a 46-year-old man, who under the influence of alcohol, had dialled the police control room helpline number on Tuesday around noon and claimed that there will be bomb blasts in Mumbai.Ajay Shinde, senior inspector of APMC police station said, "The accused identified as Tufan Sheikh was traced by tracking his mobile tower location. He had dialled from near Visava hotel in sector 19, APMC area. Considering the sensitivity of the case, the Bomb Detection and Disposal Squad (BDDS) was also summoned. He was traced and apprehended. He claimed that under the influence of alcohol, he made a hoax call of bomb blast.He works as a contract worker in APMC area. The accused has been booked under relevant sections of BNS for the offence of false information, public mischief and criminal intimidation. As per the provision of the law, he has been served with a BNSS notice and released."You Can Also Check:Gold Rate in Mumbai|Silver Rate in Mumbai|Bank Holidays in Mumbai|Public Holidays in Mumbai|Mumbai AQI|Weather in Mumbai Hot PicksShreyas Iyer SisterUpdated IPL Points TablePurple cap winnerOrange cap winnerIPL Points TablePublic holidays April 2026Bank Holidays April Shreyas Iyer SisterUpdated IPL Points TablePurple cap winnerOrange cap winnerIPL Points TablePublic holidays April 2026Bank Holidays April Top TrendingTaylor Swift and Travis Kelce RelationshipIPL 2026Donald TrumpB V NagarathnaRomarioMHT CET Admit cardBengal PollIPL Points TableSchool Holidays in AprilKarnataka 2nd PUC Exam Result Date Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce RelationshipIPL 2026Donald TrumpB V NagarathnaRomarioMHT CET Admit cardBengal PollIPL Points TableSchool Holidays in AprilKarnataka 2nd PUC Exam Result Date Tired of too many ads?go ad free nowTrending StoriesIn CityEntire WebsiteED attaches properties worth Rs 944 cr of Piyush Colonisers Ltd, ex-promotersSUV rams IndiGo plane engine at Kolkata airport; articles.title: Lateral entry, specialised allied subject could fetch 2 degrees for 4-yr graduates
articles.content: Mumbai: Students pursuing the fourth year of undergraduate programmes will be awarded two degrees upon completion if they opt for lateral admission to another college or university department, or if they undertake their fourth year in an allied subject.The first will be a regular bachelor's degree, awarded after exiting the three-year programme in their core subject, and the second an honours degree in the same or allied subject.In a Mumbai University meeting this week, colleges were also informed students can choose to graduate with either an Honours or Honours with Research degree or a postgraduate diploma, depending on their preference after four years. While the meeting was to clarify guidelines for four-year programmes under National Education Policy (NEP), principals added the idea of awarding two degrees could sound âbizarre' and needed more clarity.A circular to this effect is yet to be issued.A principal said, "Technically, a BCom accounting and finance (BAF) student, for instance, should be allowed to exit after three years with a BAF degree and on completion of the fourth year with a BAF (honours). Now, as colleges do not have a postgraduate course after BAF, the university is suggesting giving the honours degree in an allied subject such as in finance, or even financial management," he said, adding they need more clarity.The principal said the university could have allowed colleges to start the MAF programme instead this year.University officials, though, said multidisciplinary learning is the essence of NEP."Admissions to the fourth year should be considered as admissions to the master's programme. If an MCom in financial management was open to all undergraduates in finance, irrespective of their core subjects, the same should be applicable now.All colleges and university departments offering PG courses will be allowed to offer fourth-year honours and honours with research programmes, if they fulfil the eligibility criteria," said the official.Another official said with NEP, the concept of two degrees is not unusual. "If a student exited after two years, a diploma was awarded and if the same student returned to complete the honours programme, he will be awarded the honours degree too," he said.You Can Also Check:Gold Rate in Mumbai|Silver Rate in Mumbai|Bank Holidays in Mumbai|Public Holidays in Mumbai|Mumbai AQI|Weather in Mumbai Hot PicksShreyas Iyer SisterUpdated IPL Points TablePurple cap winnerOrange cap winnerIPL Points TablePublic holidays April 2026Bank Holidays April Shreyas Iyer SisterUpdated IPL Points TablePurple cap winnerOrange cap winnerIPL Points TablePublic holidays April 2026Bank Holidays April Top TrendingTaylor Swift and Travis Kelce RelationshipIPL 2026Donald TrumpB V NagarathnaRomarioMHT CET Admit cardBengal PollIPL Points TableSchool Holidays in AprilKarnataka 2nd PUC Exam Result Date Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce RelationshipIPL 2026Donald TrumpB V NagarathnaRomarioMHT CET Admit cardBengal PollIPL Points TableSchool Holidays in AprilKarnataka 2nd PUC Exam Result Date Tired of too many ads?go ad free nowTrending StoriesIn CityEntire WebsiteED attaches properties worth Rs 944 cr of Piyush Colonisers Ltd, ex-promotersSUV rams IndiGo plane engine at Kolkata airport; articles.title: On Day One, robust response to Mumbai Metro 9, tepid for Line 2B
articles.content: Mumbai: The first day of the Phase 1 stretches of Metro Line 9 (KashigaonâDahisar) and Line 2B (MandaleâDiamond Garden, Chembur) saw contrasting commuter response. While the Mira-Bhayander corridor attracted strong footfalls due to immediate travel gains, the eastern suburban line witnessed a subdued turnout because of its limited present-day connectivity.The ridership was so low in Line 2B that a staffer at one of the stations said that till 4.30pm, barely 250 people had travelled on this route since it started in the morning.Commuters on Metro 9, now seamlessly integrated with Line 7 up to Gundavali, said the new corridor is already shaving 15 to 20 minutes off daily journeys while offering a far more comfortable alternative to road travel and overcrowded suburban trains.A Metro One (Versova-Andheri-Ghatkopar) official said, "On day 1 of the opening of Metro 9, positive ridership trends were observed in all 5 stations between WEH and Saki Naka." Metro 2A and 7 have had a combined average per day ridership of 3.4 lakh. It is expected to rise by at least 1 lakh following the commissioning of Line 9.For many residents of Mira Road and Bhayander, the biggest relief is escaping the chronic traffic bottleneck at Dahisar check naka."This is a huge relief. Earlier, reaching Dahisar itself used to be a struggle. Now, I can board the metro at Kashigaon and reach Andheri far more comfortably," said Hari Pal, a logistics professional who commutes to Andheri East.Vaibhav Singh, a resident of Jesal Park in Bhayander East, said he earlier spent nearly 45 minutes just reaching Andheri via Bhayander station. "Now, I can get to Kashigaon in 10 to 15 minutes and then take the metro directly.It saves time and is far more comfortable than buses, which often take over an hour."Two students from Mira Road who travel to a school in Dahisar told TOI the metro is far more economical than the Rs 60 autorickshaw fare.Samadhan Patil, travelling with his family to Sanjay Gandhi National Park, said, "It's faster, more convenient and ideal." Pradeep Shetty, a hospitality professional from Mira Road, said, "Earlier I had to take an auto to Dahisar and then board Metro 7.This direct connectivity to Andheri will help many," he said.Several regular commuters described the service as "better late than never", saying it finally offers relief from crowded local trains at Mira Road and Dahisar stations. Women commuters said they don't have to wait for buses.However, some pointed to safety concerns, saying platform screen doors would improve passenger security. At present, guards with whistles are stationed on platforms.In contrast, the first stretch of Metro 2B received a muted response. Several commuters said the corridor, in its current form, is yet to become a practical choice for daily office travel as it neither connects with the monorail nor links major commercial hubs.Navi Mumbai's Arindam Mahapatra, an infrastructure enthusiast, said, "The line's true potential will unfold only after it is extended to crucial interchange and business districts such as Kurla, Bandra and Andheri. Once these nodes are connected, Line 2B is likely to emerge as a key east-west mobility corridor."Unlike in the past, when agencies routinely shared day one ridership figures for metro corridors, at the time of going to press, MMRDA had not released commuter numbers.You Can Also Check:Gold Rate in Mumbai|Silver Rate in Mumbai|Bank Holidays in Mumbai|Public Holidays in Mumbai|Mumbai AQI|Weather in Mumbai Hot PicksShreyas Iyer SisterUpdated IPL Points TablePurple cap winnerOrange cap winnerIPL Points TablePublic holidays April 2026Bank Holidays April Shreyas Iyer SisterUpdated IPL Points TablePurple cap winnerOrange cap winnerIPL Points TablePublic holidays April 2026Bank Holidays April Top TrendingTaylor Swift and Travis Kelce RelationshipIPL 2026Donald TrumpB V NagarathnaRomarioMHT CET Admit cardBengal PollIPL Points TableSchool Holidays in AprilKarnataka 2nd PUC Exam Result Date Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce RelationshipIPL 2026Donald TrumpB V NagarathnaRomarioMHT CET Admit cardBengal PollIPL Points TableSchool Holidays in AprilKarnataka 2nd PUC Exam Result Date Tired of too many ads?go ad free nowTrending StoriesIn CityEntire WebsiteED attaches properties worth Rs 944 cr of Piyush Colonisers Ltd, ex-promotersSUV rams IndiGo plane engine at Kolkata airport; articles.title: SIT to seek Kharat custody in third rape case today
articles.content: Nashik: Thespecial investigation team(SIT) investigating cases involving astrologer Ashok Kharat will seek his police custody in the third case on Thursday.A court sent him to 14 days of judicial remand after his custody with the SIT over the second case ended on Wednesday.Due to security concerns, Kharat was not produced in court in person and the proceedings were held via video-conference.The SIT sought the court's permission to arrest him in the third case filed at Sarkarwada police station in Nashik. Police said the case relates to rape and outraging modesty, punishable under relevant sections of BNS and other sections of Maharashtra Prevention of Black Magic Act. The complainant also accused Kharat of money laundering and scaring people with toy snakes and tigers to extort money.Police officials said he'll be taken to Nashik Road jail and re-arrested there in the third case. articles.title: Rape case: HC gives pre-arrest bail to ex-BF
articles.content: Mumbai: Bombay HC recently gave anticipatory bail to a 30-year-old accused of raping his former girlfriend.Justice Nitin Borkar said, "It appears that though in March 2024 itself, the [27-year-old woman] got to know about the marriage, she lodged the [complaint] on May 9, 2025."He said "the alleged physical relationship... appears to be consensual."The prosecution said the two came in contact through Instagram. In Sept 2019, the man called her to his house and on the alleged assurance of marriage, he persuaded her to have sex then and on multiple occasions. In 2023, he went to the US. In March 2024, she came across his wedding photos online.The man's advocate, Shashikant Chandak, said when he got married, they were no longer dating. In April 2025, she sent him a legal notice seeking an unconditional apology. When he did not, the false FIR was lodged, said Chandak. articles.title: Auto drivers protest at Mumbai RTO; services affected in suburbs
articles.content: Mumbai: Auto drivers across Maharashtra staged a morcha at Regional Transport Office (RTO) premises on Wednesday, protesting what unions have called an unjustified fee structure for joining the state welfare scheme for autorickshaw drivers.In Mumbai, the main protest was held outside the Andheri RTO. This also impacted auto services in the western suburbs to some extent as drivers were part of the protest.At the centre of the protest is the requirement that each driver pay Rs 500 as a joining fee and Rs 300 as an annual fee to enrol in the welfare scheme, taking the total to Rs 800. Driver representatives said the charges defeat the very purpose of a welfare board, which was set up around a year ago to support workers in the sector. articles.title: Thane Man Duped of ₹22 Lakh in Railway Job Scam
articles.content: Thane: A 56-year-old resident from the Balkum area was allegedly defrauded of Rs 22 lakh in a sophisticated job racket promising high-ranking positions in the Indian Railways. The fraud, which unfolded over a period of nine months between Aug 2024 and May 2025, involved the use of forged govt documents and official insignias to win the victim's trust.According to the police, the perpetrators reportedly lured the victim with the prospect of securing employment as an âInspector of Post' and a âRail Supervisor.' To bolster the deception, they sent several counterfeit appointment letters via digital platforms, complete with the Indian Railways logo and the official State Emblem (Rajmudra). Under the guise of processing fees and various administrative requirements for the job, the victim was pressured into transferring a total of Rs 22 lakh through online banking and money transfer services.The victim eventually realised the scam when the promised jobs never materialised and the individuals involved refused to return the funds. Following a preliminary inquiry, the Kapurbawdi police registered a formal case under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, citing charges related to cheating, forgery and criminal conspiracy. articles.title: Cong keeps NCP guessing over decision to withdraw nominee
articles.content: Pune: The deadline ends on Thursday but the Congress has kept the NCP guessing about its decision on withdrawing its candidate from the Baramati bypoll contest.Of the 53 candidates in the fray, six independentswithdrew their nominations on Wednesday.NCP functionaries have contacted the other candidates, requesting them to withdraw. The party's senior leadership continued its efforts, appealing to the Congress to withdraw its candidate to ensure an unopposed win for deputy chief minister Sunetra Pawar from the Baramati seat which fell vacant after the death of her husband and then deputy chief minister Ajit Pawar in plane crash on Jan 28.The efforts by NCP members have not yet yielded positive results so far as the Congress has stuck to its stand.NCP chief Sunetra Pawar, state party president Sunil Tatkare, cabinet minister Hasan Mushrif and Rajya Sabha MP Parth Pawar met chief minister Devendra Fadnavis to discuss the Baramati bypoll.Though some BJP functionaries claimed that some party seniors were planning to speak to state Congress functionaries to request them to withdraw their candidate from Baramati, the state Congress, on its official X handle, claimed that no BJP functionary has contacted the party with the request.You Can Also Check:Gold Rate in Mumbai|Silver Rate in Mumbai|Bank Holidays in Mumbai|Public Holidays in Mumbai|Mumbai AQI|Weather in Mumbai Hot PicksShreyas Iyer SisterUpdated IPL Points TablePurple cap winnerOrange cap winnerIPL Points TablePublic holidays April 2026Bank Holidays April Shreyas Iyer SisterUpdated IPL Points TablePurple cap winnerOrange cap winnerIPL Points TablePublic holidays April 2026Bank Holidays April Top TrendingTaylor Swift and Travis Kelce RelationshipIPL 2026Donald TrumpB V NagarathnaRomarioMHT CET Admit cardBengal PollIPL Points TableSchool Holidays in AprilKarnataka 2nd PUC Exam Result Date Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce RelationshipIPL 2026Donald TrumpB V NagarathnaRomarioMHT CET Admit cardBengal PollIPL Points TableSchool Holidays in AprilKarnataka 2nd PUC Exam Result Date Tired of too many ads?go ad free nowTrending StoriesIn CityEntire WebsiteED attaches properties worth Rs 944 cr of Piyush Colonisers Ltd, ex-promotersSUV rams IndiGo plane engine at Kolkata airport; articles.title: Firing at RTI activist: Stolen motorcycle used by shooter found
articles.summary: Crime branch unit-1 has recovered the motorcycle used in the Airoli firing incident where RTI activist Amit Maurya was shot near his neck. The stolen bike was found abandoned in Mumbra. Police are actively tracing the helmet-clad shooter, with the motive behind the murder attempt yet to be confirmed.
articles.content: MUMBAI: The crime branch unit-1 recovered the motorcycle used in the Airoli firing in which RTI activist Amit Maurya, 40, sustained a bullet injury near his neck on Monday night. The bike was found on the roadside in Mumbra, reports George Mendonca.On Monday, at around 11.45 pm, Maurya and his friend Sandeep Gavas, 44, were standing near the parked MUV of Gavas when a biker wearing a helmet approached them and, brandishing a pistol, fired in their direction. As they dodged, two bullets hit the MUVâs windscreen. Maurya and Gavas ran when the assailant fired two more rounds. One bullet pierced Mauryaâs neck near the left ear, while the other bullet fired at Gavas hit a wall on the roadside.A police source said, âThe CCTV camera footage was scanned, and the shooter riding the bike was tracked. As he was wearing a helmet, he could not be identified. However, the bikeâs registration number was caught on CCTV. It was found abandoned in Mumbra in the wee hours of Wednesday. From the bikeâs registration number, the ownerâs details were obtained from the RTO. It was learnt that the bike was stolen from Sanpada.âMaurya underwent an operation at Fortis Hospital, Mulund, and the bullet lodged near his neck was removed. Police said his condition was stable and he was shifted from the ICU to the general ward. A police officer privy to the case, said, âAs of now, it cannot be confirmed if it was a contract killing. Only after the shooter is arrested will the motive behind the murder attempt be clear.âYou Can Also Check:Gold Rate in Mumbai|Silver Rate in Mumbai|Bank Holidays in Mumbai|Public Holidays in Mumbai|Mumbai AQI|Weather in Mumbai Hot PicksKerala VotingRaghav ChadhaPurple cap winnerOrange cap winnerIPL Points TablePublic holidays April 2026Bank Holidays April Kerala VotingRaghav ChadhaPurple cap winnerOrange cap winnerIPL Points TablePublic holidays April 2026Bank Holidays April Top TrendingPope Leo XIVMamata BanerjeePawan KheraSabarimala CaseMike VrabelMegan Thee StallionSerena WilliamsIPL Points TableSchool Holidays in AprilKarnataka 2nd PUC Exam Result Date Pope Leo XIVMamata BanerjeePawan KheraSabarimala CaseMike VrabelMegan Thee StallionSerena WilliamsIPL Points TableSchool Holidays in AprilKarnataka 2nd PUC Exam Result Date Tired of too many ads?go ad free nowTrending StoriesIn CityEntire WebsiteBTech student in Bengaluru dies after 'fall' from 9th floor of hostel; father suspects foul playâYou canât talk like this,â observer shoots back at CEC Gyanesh Kumar, gets removedWoman murdered, body found inside bed box in Delhi; 3 including lover arrestedBlack Scorpio, numberless plate, 3 gunmen: Haryana financier gunned down; over 20 rounds fired in 1 minuteInterstate thief who hid underwater for 5 hours, breathing through a lotus stem, held in Madhya PradeshKhan Chacha founder's son held for muling a/cDelhi studentsâ daily grind: Managing roti, kapda, makaan... articles.title: Maharashtra govt forms committee to define Dharavi Koliwada boundaries
articles.content: Mumbai: State revenue minister Chandrashekhar Bawankule announced that a committee headed by the Konkan divisional commissioner was set up to draw up the boundaries of Dharavi Koliwada after residents opposed the inclusion of the Koliwada in the Dharavi Redevelopment Project (DRP) and demanded that their land on which buildings were constructed be restored to them.Bawankule said the committee, comprising the BMC commissioner, Mumbai city and suburbs collectors and the land commissioner, will submit its report to the govt within three months.On Wednesday, local Congress MLA Jyoti Gaikwad led a delegation of residents to meet Bawankule. Residents said the Koliwada has been in existence since the 12th century, is a heritage precinct and should be excluded completely from the Dharavi Redevelopment Project.They also demanded that the local temple to the deity Khandoba and the nearby Holi Maidan be given the status of a religious centre.Recently, Navbharat Mega Developers Pvt Ltd (NMDPL), the special purpose vehicle set up to execute the redevelopment project, took up the barricading of Mhada-constructed govt quarters, including ONGC, AEML, BEST and GRP. Koliwada residents opposed the action, claiming the land belongs to Dharavi Koliwada.An NMDPL spokesperson said the SPV officially took possession of these buildings as they fall under the Dharavi Redevelopment area. "NMDPL is not a party to any legal dispute related to the land as all land parcels vest with the DRP/SRA. articles.title: Vietnam group’s $9 billion Maharashtra plans to create 25k jobs
articles.summary: Vietnam's Vin Group will invest $8.5 billion in Maharashtra over two years, focusing on sustainable urban development, industry, tourism, and electric mobility. The company plans to develop smart residential townships on 5,000 acres in the Mumbai Metropolitan Region, creating 24,700 jobs.
articles.content: Representative ImageMUMBAI: Vietnam-based Vin Group Joint Stock Company on Wednesday announced that it will invest around US$8.5 billion over the next two years in Maharashtra in the fields of sustainable urban development, industry and tourism, renewable energy, and electric mobility.It proposes to set up various projects on 5,000 acres in the Mumbai Metropolitan Region, and it is estimated it will create 24,700 jobs. The company on Wednesday signed a memorandum of understanding with the state industries dept and the Mumbai Metropolitan Region Development Authority (MMRDA) in the presence of chief minister Devendra Fadnavis.As per this agreement, emphasis will be placed on setting up large-scale smart residential townships.An integrated township will be developed on 2,700 acres, and a population of about two lakh has been envisaged to reside here. Along with this, it is proposed to start electric taxi services to promote electric mobility in the state, and a mobility-as-a-service platform will also be developed.Fadnavis also inaugurated the MB3 data centre of Equinix at his official residence, Varsha. The data centre is spread over 3.7 acres and has been set up with a total investment of about US$750 million (approximately Rs 6,000 crore).Once fully operational, it will have a capacity of over 4,200 cabinets and will be connected to Equinix's data centres across the world through a hybrid multi-cloud interconnection facility, said a govt press release.A large portion of the energy required for the data centre is being provided by a 26.6 MW solar project in Yavatmal. articles.title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO day 22 [LIVE]: Ranveer Singh film chases Rs. 1050 crore milestone
articles.summary: Aditya Dhar’s espionage thriller, 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge', continues its historic run at the box office. On day 22, the Ranveer Singh-starrer targets the Rs. 1,050 crore India net milestone after becoming the first Hindi film to cross the Rs. 1,000 crore mark. Despite IPL 2026, the film’s broad appeal and family-friendly updates have pushed its worldwide gross toward a staggering Rs. 1,700 crore target. Read the full day-by-day collection breakdown and live tracking here.
articles.content: Aditya Dharâs espionage thriller, 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge', continues its historic run at the box office. On day 22, the Ranveer Singh-starrer targets the Rs. 1,050 crore India net milestone after becoming the first Hindi film to cross the Rs. 1,000 crore mark. Despite IPL 2026, the filmâs broad appeal and family-friendly updates have pushed its worldwide gross toward a staggering Rs. 1,700 crore target. Read the full day-by-day collection breakdown and live tracking here.Aditya Dhar's espionage epic tightens its grip on Indian box office records as day 22 collections roll in making it the first Hindi film past Rs. 1,000 crore India net shows no signs of slowing down.The film now eyes Rs. 1,050 crore as its next landmark on this fourth Thursday since its release on March 19, 2026. The ongoing IPL 2026 season has traditionally been a graveyard for theatrical releases, diverting eyeballs to cricket in prime time. Yet 'Dhurandhar 2' has defied this trend convincingly. Analysts attribute the sustained run to its broad mass appeal, the near-universal word of mouth driven byRanveer Singh's career-best performance, and the extraordinary cultural moment around breakout star Udaybir Sandhu's viral dialogue as Pinda.Dhurandhar 2 ReviewThe makers have also made a strategic move by releasing updated digital prints with several explicit dialogues muted or removed, making the gritty spy thriller more accessible to family audiences â a decision that has visibly broadened the film's demographic reach deep into its third week.WatchHow Casting Decisions By Mukesh Chhabra Transformed Dhurandharâs Scale And Impact'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' first week and second week earningsThe film's opening week of Rs. 674.17 crore India net was nothing short of monumental, shattering every prior benchmark in Indian cinema.Leveraging the festive surge of Gudi Padwa and Eid, 'Dhurandhar 2' broke records previously held by films like 'Jawan', 'KGF: Chapter 2', and 'Baahubali 2: The Conclusion'. The film maintained incredible stamina, adding another â¹263.65 crore in its second week.By day 11 alone, collecting a massive Rs. 68.10 crore in a single day on March 29, the sequel had already crossed the entire lifetime collection of the original 2025 'Dhurandhar' Rs.840.20 crore, a feat that took the first film three months to achieve.'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' day 21 recapOn day 21, the film collected Rs. 7.90 crore India net across 13,093 shows representing a 21.8% drop from the Rs. 10.10 crore collected on day 20, a typical mid-week dip pattern for long-running blockbusters. Overseas, the film added Rs. 3.00 crore, taking total overseas gross to Rs. 407.00 crore. This pushed the worldwide gross to Rs. 1,653.67 crore, keeping the Rs.1,700 crore global target firmly in sight.'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' day 22 live trackingAccording to Sacnilk, as of day 22, the film is currently running across 8,862 shows in India. Early live tracking shows a net ofRs. 4.19 crore, with evening and night show data expected to push the daily total significantly higher once final numbers are reported.The total India gross has reachedRs. 1,251.61 crore, with India net atRs. 1,045.46 croreso far. Final day 22 collections are yet to be declared.Day and week wise collections for 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge'Week 1 collection: Rs. 674.17 croreWeek 2 collection: Rs. 263.65 croreDay 16: Rs. 21.55 croreDay 17: Rs. 25.65 croreDay 18: Rs. 28.25 croreDay 19: Rs. 10.00 croreDay 20: Rs. 10.10 croreDay 21: Rs. 7.90 croreDay 22: Rs. 4.19 croreTOTAL: Rs. 1045.46 croreLanguage wise distribution for 'Dhurandhar 2' day 22For day 22, the film's earnings across different languages show a dominant Hindi performance at Rs. 3.49 crore, while the Telugu and Tamil versions contributed Rs. 0.09 crore and Rs. 0.06 crore respectively.PollHow do you rate the box office performance of  Dhurandhar 2?ExceptionalDisappointingMore about 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge'The gritty 229-minute film follows Jaskirat Singh Rangi played by Ranveer Singh, a man shaped by personal tragedy who is recruited by R&AW and resurfaces as Hamza Ali Mazari, India's most lethal deep-cover operative who forays deep into the politics of Karachi. The ensemble cast featuresSanjay Duttas the intimidating SSP Chaudhary Aslam, Arjun Rampal as the cold ISI mastermind Major Iqbal and Rakesh Bedi as Jameel Jamali, the Pakistani politician revealed to be an Indian mole.Sara Arjunlends an emotional layer to Hamza's role while Yami Gautam appears in a cameo appearance.Meanwhile, Udaybir Sandhu's performance as Pinda and his haunting line "Ghar di yaad nahi aayi tujhe, Jassi?" have trended globally for weeks.DISCLAIMER: The box office numbers and data in this article are compiled from diverse public and industry sources. articles.title: Somy says industry people cut ties after speaking about abuse from Salman
articles.summary: Former Bollywood actor Somy Ali alleges she was boycotted by the industry after speaking out about an abusive relationship with a powerful actor. She claims prominent figures like Suniel Shetty and Vivek Oberoi stopped talking to her, suggesting a directive from the actor she accused. Ali also reflected on her unexpected entry into acting, attributing her career to fate.
articles.content: Former Bollywood actorSomy Alihas alleged that she was sidelined by the Hindi film industry after she spoke publicly about being in an abusive relationship with a powerful actor. Though she did not name him outright, Somy was previously in a long-term relationship withSalman Khanand had earlier also posted about it on social media. She had said about him, "The eight years spent with him were the worst years of my existence.In addition to tonnes of affairs and flings, he would constantly belittle me by calling me ugly, stupid, and dumb. Not a day passed that he wouldn't make me feel worthless and small." Though, she deleted this Instagram post later.Now in a recent interview, she says that after she spoke about being in this abusive relationship with a superstar actor, many people from the industry abruptly cut ties with her. She said in an interview with Money Control, âNo one took a stand when I spoke out and I've been, in fact, boycotted from Bollywood.I spoke up against him and his revenge was that he decided he was going to destroy my NGO and malign my reputation.People likeSuniel Shetty, people likeVivek Oberoi, who is like my rakhi brother, every single one of them stopped talking to me. And I was like, what happened all of a sudden?â She added that it soon became evident that âif this person says âSomy Ali se baat mat karo,â then Somy Ali se baat mat karo.âWatchSomy Aliâs Explosive Claims on Salman Khan, Aishwarya, and the Bishnoi Feud Go ViralReflecting on how she entered the film industry, Somy revealed that acting was never part of her plan.âWhy did I leave? I think everyone knows I didnât go there for acting and acting just happened. I didnât go there for acting. I went there for another reason,â she said. Despite this, she found herself working alongside some of the biggest names in Bollywood at the time.She credited destiny for her journey, saying, âI believe in fate and destiny. Whatâs supposed to happen, whatâs already written, is going to happen. You cannot change it.âSomy recalled working with actors like Sanjay Dutt, Om Puri, and Salman Khan, describing her career trajectory as something that âjust happened.âLooking back, she described herself as an inexperienced teenager navigating a complex industry. âI was a young girl who wasnât interested in acting. I always missed my dance rehearsals. I would drag Suniel Shetty from Roshan Tanejaâs class to Shiv Sagar and say, âLetâs go eat pav bhaji.â And I ended up working with the top actors. Om Puri ji in Mississippi Masala. It was crazy. articles.title: When Abhishek revealed why Jaya Bachchan gets angry at the paps
articles.summary: Jaya Bachchan's birthday prompts a look at her paparazzi interactions. While often seen as stern, friends recall her warmth. Her children, Abhishek and Shweta, revealed her claustrophobia and dislike for unasked photos as reasons for her reactions. Despite this, she has shown a softer side, delighting fans with her rare smiles.
articles.content: AsJaya Bachchancelebrates her birthday, itâs a good moment to revisit her often-discussed equation with the paparazzi. While she is frequently perceived as stern or irritated in such interactions, many still remember her as the warm and charming âGuddiâ.People who know the actress feel she's one of the warmest people they've met. Not to mention, while her current public persona is largely to do with her relationship with the paps, her iconic movies and the benchmark she's set as an actress with unique choices, must be remembered.Over the years, her reactions to photographers have sparked conversations, but her children,Abhishek Bachchanand Shweta Bachchan Nanda, once shed light on the real reason behind it during an appearance on 'Koffee With Karan'.WatchAmitabh Bachchan May Call Marriage His Biggest Mistake, Jaya Bachchan Jokes!When asked about navigating public appearances with her, Abhishek responded with his trademark humour, even bringing in Amitabh Bachchan andAishwarya Rai Bachchaninto the joke. He said, "If it's like the four of us, Dad, Aishwarya and I just hold hands, say a silent prayer before the red carpet. And then we collect ourselves and walk on. If Shweta Di is with us, we just send her with Maa."Adding a more serious perspective, Shweta explained what really triggers her motherâs discomfort.She said, "She gets very claustrophobic when there are too many people around her. She also doesn't like it when people take her pictures without asking her. She comes from that school of thought."Despite her occasional stern moments, Jaya Bachchan has also been seen interacting warmly with photographers on several occasions, showing a different side of her personality. articles.title: Sooraj reunites with Himesh for Ayushmann and Sharvari film
articles.summary: Himesh Reshammiya is reuniting with director Sooraj Barjatya for a new family entertainer starring Ayushmann Khurrana and Sharvari. Following their successful collaboration on 'Prem Ratan Dhan Payo', Himesh has delivered seven simple, emotional, and relatable songs designed to resonate with audiences across India, including rural areas. The film's shoot is underway.
articles.content: Salman Khan andSonam Kapoorteamed up in 2015 for the film 'Prem Ratan Dhan Payo'. Apart from the decent story line and performances,Himesh Reshammiya's songs were the highlight. Now, the latest reports indicate thatSooraj Barjatyais working with Himesh Reshammiya once more for his new family entertainer starringAyushmann Khurranaand Sharvari. Himesh has already delivered seven songs for the project, continuing the successful partnership they had on 'Prem Ratan Dhan Payo'.The director wanted each track to be simple, emotional, and easy for everyone to connect with, not just city audiences but also people in smaller towns and villages.WatchSooraj Barjatya Exclusive: Next 'Prem', New Salman Khan Film & OTT Debut With 'Bada Naam Karenge'Why was Himesh Reshammiya chosen?As reported by Bollywood Hungama, Sooraj Barjatya felt Himesh was the right fit for the kind of music he had in mind. Both had enjoyed working together before, and Sooraj knew Himesh could create tunes that reach far beyond metros. Himesh was happy to come on board and has given exactly what the director asked for: songs that feel warm and relatable.The songs are being made to work across different sections of the audience. Sooraj wanted them to be soulful and mass-appealing so they could travel to rural areas as well. Himesh, known for making music that connects with a wide range of listeners, has delivered tracks that match this brief reportedly.Shoot progress for the Ayushmann Khurrana starrerThe filmâs shooting started on November 1, 2025, and is expected to finish in about a month. Ayushmann Khurrana and Sharvari are leading the cast in what is being described as a heartfelt family story.Soorajâs Barjatya's signature styleMusic has always been an important part of Sooraj Barjatyaâs films. His movies are remembered for their emotional songs that families enjoy together. This new collaboration with Himesh is expected to bring back that familiar warmth and simplicity in the music. Meanwhile, 'Prem Ratan Dhan Payo' was written and directed by Sooraj Barjatya and Salman Khan played a dual role in the comedy entertainer film.WatchRani Mukerji Stuns in Ethnic Wear at Sooraj Barjatyaâs Daughterâs WeddingGet the latest entertainment updates from the Times of India, along with thelatest Hindi movies,upcoming Hindi movies in 2026, andTelugu movies.â Tired of too many ads?go ad free nowentertainment videos03:32Samay Rainaâs âThisâ Remark Backfires, Mukesh Khanna Hits Back Over Shaktimaan Comment03:32Samay Rainaâs âThisâ Remark Backfires, Mukesh Khanna Hits Back Over Shaktimaan Comment06:12Royal Shock: Andrew Accused of Palace 'Brawl' As New Book Reveals Bullying Allegations07:05Shahid Kapoor Upset With Screen Time? articles.title: Manav Gohil on why 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' is nearly four hours long
articles.summary: Manav Gohil discussed the unusually long runtime of 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge', attributing it to director Aditya Dhar's insistence on retaining all researched details. Gohil also revealed he was unaware of the sequel until the first film's release and humorously noted his family's increased interest after the movie's success, despite his limited screen time.
articles.content: 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' has already become one of the biggest successes of recent times. At a time when audience attention spans are very low, theRanveer Singhstarrer managed to keep the audiences engaged despite its long runtime.Manav Gohil, who played IB deputy director Bansal in 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge', opened up about the filmâs unusually long runtime. Both parts of the franchise are among the longest Bollywood films in recent years; the second part runs nearly four hours. In a recent interview, the actor explained that directorAditya Dharwas very particular about retaining all researched information, even if it made the film lengthy.Manav Gohil on Aditya Dharâs strict approachIn an interview with News 18, Manav Gohil said Aditya Dhar refused to compromise on details. He shared, âThereâs so much information in the film. Aditya was very strict about not getting rid of any detail. What we had at hand was hardcore research-based information. He said, âYeh sab dikhaana zaroori hai. He didnât compromise on it. He was so tuned in all the way down to the minutest nitty-gritties of the storytelling.And people loved all of it."WatchPathaan Mocked As Dhurandhar Dominates Bollywood; SRK-Salman Vs Ranveer Sparks Generational DebateThe actor admitted the team felt they had shot a lot, but Aditya was firm about keeping everything.Manav Gohil had no prior knowledge of the sequelDuring the same interaction, Gohil revealed he was not told about a second part until much later. He said, âWe all felt ki humne bahot zyada shoot kar liya hai. Hum shoot kiye hi ja rahe the, kiye hi ja rahe the (laughs). We knew that it was going to turn out to be a long film but we didnât anticipate that it will be divided into two parts.We came to know of it when we were about to release the first film. Thatâs when we got a sense and were officially told that Dhurandhar: The Revenge will also be released."Gohil revealed that he found out about the sequel only when the first film was nearing release.Familyâs reaction to 'Dhurandhar'Manav Gohil also opened up about how his family, especially his wife and actress Shweta Kawatra, reacted to 'Dhurandhar'. Gohil said, âIt was so difficult to watch it with Shweta.She had so many questions to ask me. I had to tell her that she needs to let me watch the film first for me to understand and answer all her questions." He joked that after the filmâs release, he suddenly became âimportantâ for his family, with everyone asking him about the story during dinners.Manav Gohil on screen time feedbackThe actor also addressed the common complaint from fans about his limited screen time. He laughed and said, âMujhe bhi aisa hi laga ki mera screen time kam kyun hai (laughs). And though people reacted very sweetly to my scenes and they told me that they loved my performance, they asked me, aur bhi hai kya?" (âI also felt the same like, why is my screen time so less? (laughs). And though people reacted very sweetly to my scenes and said they loved my performance, they kept asking, âIs there more?ââ)Despite the short role, his performance as IB deputy director Bansal was appreciated by audiences. articles.title: 'Beef Season 2': Mulligan & Isaac craft ‘big old puzzle’ fight
articles.summary: Get ready for 'Beef' Season 2, promising more intense drama and raw emotion. Stars Carey Mulligan and Oscar Isaac reveal their explosive fight scene was meticulously planned like a 'big old puzzle.' The new season shifts to two couples caught in a wealthy couple's escalating conflict, exploring class divides and manipulation. The show premieres April 16, 2026, on Netflix.
articles.content: âBeefâ is coming back, and this time, itâs ramping everything up: more tension, more chaos, and a whole lot more raw emotion. As âBeef Season 2â gets closer, people arenât just talking about its new cast; theyâre buzzing about the scenes that promise to get under the audienceâs skin.Right in the middle of this spotlight areCarey MulliganandOscar Isaac. They recently sat down to talk about filming one of the new seasonâs standout moments: a fight scene they say felt like âa big old puzzle.âInside the âBeef Season 2â fight sceneAccording to Deadline, at the Season 2 premiere in Los Angeles, Carey Mulligan and Oscar Isaac discussed the work that went into their explosive brawl in the first episode. From the beginning, they knew it had to feel true, not just flashy.No wild improvising, no cheap dramaâthey mapped everything out, making sure every move and every beat tied to what these characters would actually feel and do.Mulligan said, âThe fight scene was a big old puzzle that we took a long time to figure out with Sonny [creator Lee Sung Jin], over months.â That scene isnât just a simple argument; itâs a domestic clash that gets uncomfortable fast, tapping into those bigger âBeefâ themes: resentment, pride, how we keep messing up when trying to connect.Isaac, who plays Josh, Mulliganâs husband in the show, talked about the process: âWe did improvisations, we did rehearsals where we taped out the thing, almost like a theater play.â They spent a lot of time blocking out where to stand and move, where the cameras would go, what every pause and glance should feel like. It looks full of chaos on screen, but underneath, everything is in its right place.Because this fight kicks off everything for the new season, Mulligan said the stakes felt huge: âthe onus was on it being very truthful.âShe admitted they barely knew these new characters at first, so keeping the fight interesting and real was tough. And then, when their kids end up witnessing the aftermath, it was a tough thing to pull off. She credited Sonny Lee for working and reworking the script live, changing things up as they went, until it really landed.What happened in âBeefâ Season 1?In case you missed the first season, hereâs a quick catch-up. âBeefâ is the brainchild ofLee Sung Jin. In the first season, the story followed Danny (Steven Yeun) and Amy (Ali Wong), two strangers whose explosive road rage moment turns into an all-consuming obsession.The mix of dark comedy, biting drama, and an honest take on modern anger made it a big hit, and it picked up four Emmys and three Golden Globes, including best limited series.What happens in âBeef Season 2â?Now, for Season 2, âBeefâ is shifting gears. Itâs turned into an anthology, meaning fresh faces, a brand new story, and totally new conflicts. This time, itâs about two couples: A young, working-class pair (played by Charles Melton and Cailee Spaeny) stumble into the middle of a violent fight between their wealthy boss (Isaac) and his wife (Mulligan) at a country club.That one moment spirals out, dragging everyone into a mess of manipulation, blackmail, and rising tension.The show moves into the world of country club elites, Korean billionaires, class divides, and shifting alliances. Secrets start coming out, lines get crossed, and you can feel things tightening with every episode. âBeef Season 2â drops all eight episodes on Netflix on April 16, 2026. articles.title: Euphoria S03: Zendaya’s late arrival fuels Sydney Sweeney feud
articles.summary: The launch event for 'Euphoria Season 3' was rife with intrigue as Zendaya and Sydney Sweeney opted to arrive independently, igniting chatter about a possible fallout. Insiders hint that their friendship may be strained, possibly due to factors like Tom Holland's involvement and varying political stances.
articles.content: The âEuphoria Season 3â premiere shouldâve been a big, glittery reunion. Fans waited four years for this moment, but instead, everyone talked aboutZendayaand Sydney Sweeney, specifically, whether Zendaya showed up late just to dodge Sydney due to ongoing feud rumors.So, what actually happened? Letâs unpack.What happened at the âEuphoria Season 3â premiere?According to Page Six, the âEuphoria Season 3â premiere took place in Los Angeles on Tuesday night. Most of the cast turned heads on the red carpet: Jacob Elordi, Hunter Schafer, Alexa Demie, and Maude Apatow all looked sharp and stylish. Sydney Sweeney arrived first, wearing a dramatic white bridal gown that went viral in minutes. The rest of the castmates were around by then.They posed on the red carpet and appeared for interviews as well.However, Zendaya arrived much later, after most of the cast had already walked, and skipped parts of the event, including the official after-party. People noticed. She barely missed running into Sydney, and Jacob Elordi also showed up late, skipping interviews and only posing for photos on the red carpet. Per the outlet, Zendaya and Sydney didnât take photos together.There was no sign of them chatting or interacting in public.Later, Zendaya skipped the after-party at Chateau Marmont, which made the distance between them more obvious, especially since they used to pose together at past premieres.WatchZendaya Responds To Tom Holland Secret Wedding Rumors On Jimmy Kimmel LiveJust a few years ago, Zendaya and Sweeney posed together at the Season 1 and Season 2 premieres, and Sweeney praised Zendayaâs leadership during an Access Hollywood interview in 2022, saying, âIâm really lucky because last season I didnât get to work with her as much.âSweeney sang praises for the âEuphoriaâ lead, and added, âItâs been amazing to be able to watch her and to see how much of a boss [she is].âThe rumored âfeudâ between Zendaya and Sydney Sweeney: What about it?According to The Sun, there have been whispers about tension between Zendaya and Sydney for months, but neither has said anything publicly. At this premiere, they kept their distance, and insiders claim thatâs not news to anyone close to the show.One source told the outlet, ââEuphoriaâ bosses know the pair donât get along.There were tensions way back. During Season 3, their paths barely crossed.â Apparently, the press tour is tightly managed to avoid awkward moments, and journalists arenât allowed to ask about their relationship.TheTom Hollandfactor and the politicsAccording to insiders, Sydney was openly flirty with Tom when he visited the set, which didnât sit well with Zendaya. After that, the two actresses stopped hanging out together.Zendaya and Tom Holland, both 29, reportedly got married, but havenât confirmed it.Over the years, whatever tension there was seems to have grown. Last month, fans noticed Sydney was missing from a group photo promoting the new season, which only added fuel to the fire. Another insider said, âThereâll be a sigh of relief when âEuphoriaâ is over and they can go their separate ways. But this rivalry isnât going anywhere; they both have long careers ahead.âThen, politics plays a part too. Sydney is a registered Republican, and thatâs become another wedge.Zendaya made her opinions clear when she posted âVote this MF outâ about Trump in 2020. Two years later, Sydney shared photos from her momâs birthday, where party guests wore MAGA-style hats.It didnât stop there. In July, Sydney got a shout-out from Trump after her American Eagle denim ad stirred up a debate about beauty standards and âwoke culture.â Trump wrote on Truth Social, âSydney Sweeney, a registered Republican, has the âHOTTESTâ ad out there. The jeans are âflying off the shelves.â Go get âem Sydney!âAbout âEuphoria Season 3âAmid the drama, the show, âEuphoriaâ, itself remains highly anticipated.After years of delays due to industry strikes and scheduling conflicts, the new season marks a significant shift for the series. Unlike the first two seasons, which focused on high school life, Season 3 introduces a time jump, following the characters as young adults navigating life beyond adolescence.The show continues to explore addiction, identity, trauma, and relationshipsâall elements that have made it one of the most talked-about dramas. âEuphoria Season 3â premieres on April 12, 2026, on HBO and will be available for streaming on HBO Max.
