articles.title: Suzanne hits back at Deepika for not openly praising Ranveer for Dhurandhar 2
articles.summary: Online buzz erupted over Deepika Padukone's perceived silence on husband Ranveer Singh's hit film 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge'. While Deepika playfully dismissed speculation, actress Suzanne Bernert publicly questioned her lack of praise. Deepika clarified she watched the movie early, suggesting overanalysis. Despite the debate, the film continues its box-office success.
articles.content: Deepika Padukonerecently found herself at the centre of conversations on social media after she finally broke silence onRanveer Singhâs blockbuster 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge'. Ever since the film has released, the internet just cannot stop gushing about theAditya Dhardirectorial and Ranveer's performance in the film. Many have also called it his 'career-best' and how he has now come into a different league of stardom after the film's release.Deepika commented on a viral reel questioning her silence and absence from the film's screening.Dhurandhar 2 ReviewNow after Deepika reacted to this video, actressSuzanne Bernertquestioning her stance publicly. She took to social media to call out Deepikaâs reaction, expressing confusion over why the actress had not openly praised her husbandâs success. In her post, she wrote, âDear Deepika, This isnât a flex! We are still waiting for you to openly praise your husband.I donât understand why you are not doing it. #Dhurandhar2âTheRevenge.âFor those not in the know, Deepika responded to had claimed, âDeepika Padukone just gave the âsilent treatmentâ to a Rs 500 crore budget. While Dhurandhar 2 shatters global records, Deepika skipped the premiere to hit a sitar concert with her in-laws instead. No posts, no praise, just silence. Is she making a statement against the director or just dodging the internetâs favorite drama?âWatchDeepika Padukone Addresses Buzz Around Her Silence, Response Gains Attention OnlineDeepika responded to the video and said, "The latter my friendâ¦ P.S. I watched it way before any of you did. Now who is the joke on? (hand over mouth emoji).âEven as the debate continues on the internet, 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' remains a box-office juggernaut, with Ranveer earning widespread acclaim for his performance. articles.title: 'AK 64': Ajith's next film with Adhik stalled over salary demand
articles.summary: Ajith Kumar's highly anticipated 'AK 64' project faces an unexpected delay, reportedly due to ongoing salary negotiations between the actor and producers. Despite the star's return to Chennai and earlier indications of filming commencement, the remuneration discussions have become a sticking point. Fans are eagerly awaiting an official update on the film's progress.
articles.content: Ajith Kumar's highly anticipated 'AK 64' project faces an unexpected delay, reportedly due to ongoing salary negotiations between the actor and producers. Despite the star's return to Chennai and earlier indications of filming commencement, the remuneration discussions have become a sticking point. Fans are eagerly awaiting an official update on the film's progress.Ajith Kumarreturned to Chennai a few weeks ago. He was expected to begin work on his next film, tentatively titled 'AK 64.' However, reports suggest the project has not started yet due to issues related to the actorâs salary.This has created fresh buzz in the industry, as fans eagerly await an official update on his next big film.Ajith's salary talks become a sticking pointAccording to a report by Filmibeat, the delay is linked to salary discussions between Ajith and the producers. The actor is reportedly firm about his remuneration, and talks are still ongoing. It was noted earlier that Ajith is being offered Rs 170 to 180 crore as remuneration for his upcoming film. This count is compensated by two Ajith films in 2025, which were released in the first half of the year. 'Vidaamuyarchi,' directed byMagizh Thirumeniwas released in February but was not a big box office success. Later, 'Good Bad Ugly,' directed byAdhik Ravichandran, hit screens on April 10 and performed well commercially.WatchAjith Kumar Breaks Silence On Rivalry With Vijay, Asks The Miscreants To 'Remain Quiet'!Adhik Ravichandran likely to direct 'AK 64'After the success of 'Good Bad Ugly,' it was widely reported that Adhik Ravichandran would direct Ajithâs 64th film. The project is expected to be backed by the producer known for films like 'Nenokkadine' and 'Sarileru Neekevaru' starringMahesh Babu. Though Adhik had earlier hinted that shooting would begin soon, filming has not officially started, even after the end of March.Fans await clarity on 'AK 64'With Ajith recently returned to Chennai, many believed the film would begin shooting immediately. However, the reported salary issue seems to have slowed things down. Fans are now expressing concern over the lack of updates regarding 'AK 64.'
